examples.title: Basic Try-Catch-Throw Example
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental exception handling with try, catch, and throw keywords
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates basic exception handling: try block contains code that may fail, throw creates an exception when division by zero is attempted, and catch blocks handle the thrown exception.

examples.title: Multiple Catch Blocks
examples.description: Shows handling different exception types with multiple catch handlers
examples.explanation: Multiple catch blocks handle different exception types. The most specific exceptions (out_of_range, invalid_argument) should come before general ones (exception). The catch(...) catches any unhandled exceptions.

examples.title: Custom Exception Class
examples.description: Demonstrates creating and using user-defined exception classes
examples.explanation: Custom exception classes inherit from std::exception and override the what() method. InsufficientFundsException provides additional data (balance, withdrawal amount) for detailed error information.

examples.title: Stack Unwinding Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how local objects are destroyed during exception propagation
examples.explanation: This demonstrates stack unwinding: when exception is thrown in level3, destructors of level3, level2, and level1 resources are called in reverse order, ensuring proper cleanup of local objects.
